business.headline: From ITR filing to TDS: 3 major tax deadlines in July 2026 you shouldn't missWhether you're a salaried employee, a business owner or an employer, keeping track of these dates can help you avoid last-minute stress. Here are three key tax deadlines you should not miss in July 2026.

Will your family get your EPF pension after your death? Here's what you need to knowNo one likes to think about what happens after they're gone. But if you're an EPF member, knowing whether your family can continue receiving your pension could make a big difference to their financial future.

Will DA rise before the 8th Pay Commission? Here's what the latest data suggestsThe wait for the 8th Pay Commission continues, but all eyes are now on the next DA revision. As inflation remains elevated and the July review draws closer, is another DA hike just around the corner?

Govt restores commercial LPG supply to pre-West Asia crisis levelsThe move comes after the government reviewed the country's fuel availability and found that both domestic production and imported LPG supplies have improved.

AI reality check hits Asia as Seoul's Kospi crashes 8%, Tokyo's Nikkei tumbles 5%South Korea's KOSPI fell 8.2%, prompting authorities to halt program trading for 20 minutes after steep losses rippled across the market. Japan's Nikkei 225 dropped 5%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 2.4%.

What's the one big hurdle delaying India-US trade deal? Piyush Goyal explainsPiyush Goyal said India will bring the US trade deal into force only after Washington creates a tariff framework that favours Indian exporters over regional competitors. The position follows US legal changes that overturned reciprocal tariffs and forced both sides to revisit implementation.

Sebi may allow celebrity endorsements for financial brands. But here's a catchUnder the proposal, celebrities would be allowed to endorse a financial firm's brand or company name, subject to prescribed conditions, disclaimers and prior approvals.

Stock market holiday on Muharram: Are NSE and BSE open for trading on June 26?Planning to trade on Friday, June 26? With Muharram being observed across the country, many investors are wondering whether the NSE and BSE will remain open for trading?

Don't want to pay taxes online? Here's how to use the 'Pay at Bank Counter' optionNot comfortable paying taxes online? You're not alone. The Income Tax Department still allows taxpayers to pay their dues at authorised bank branches through the 'Pay at Bank Counter' facility.

India-US trade deal very close, but here's why it isn't signed yetPiyush Goyal said India and the US are close to finalising their trade deal. India wants tariff terms that give its exporters a clear edge over rivals.

Dalal Street pares gains: Sensex ends flat after climbing over 600 pointsEarly optimism driven by lower crude oil prices and a stronger rupee faded as investors booked profits, though benchmark indices still managed to end in positive territory.

Zerodha's Nithin Kamath has a warning for you against pyramid schemesAccording to figures cited by Kamath, more than 5.5 crore Indians have lost their savings to over 5,300 such schemes. The estimated losses stood at around Rs 10 lakh crore as of 2015, and he suggested that the actual figure could be much higher now.

Gold loses over Rs 51,000 from all-time high. Why is the yellow metal crashing?Gold has fallen sharply from its peak as silver also comes under selling pressure. The correction is being driven by rate-hike bets, a stronger dollar and shifting investor appetite.

United Spirits may cut 100 jobs as Diageo looks to trim costsUnited Spirits is set to lay off around 100 employees as Diageo pursues a global cost-cutting programme. The move comes amid a wider restructuring drive in India despite strong growth and continued investment in premium brands.

Amazon to invest $48 billion in India by 2030, CEO Andy Jassy meets PM ModiAccording to Jassy, the company aims to support 3.8 million jobs in India by 2030 and help generate $80 billion in cumulative e-commerce exports.

Sensex jumps over 600 points: Why are stock markets rallying today?Benchmark indices extended their recovery on Thursday as softer crude, firm Asian cues and gains in banks and autos lifted sentiment. The move has eased near-term macro concerns, though investors remain watchful of the monsoon and global central bank signals.

EPFO portal shutdown from June 26-28: How it will affect your EPF claimsPlanning to withdraw money from your EPF account or submit a claim this week? You may have to postpone it. EPFO has announced a three-day shutdown of key portal services as it upgrades its claims processing system.

GTA 5 made $10 billion. Here's what GTA 6 could earn if it repeats historyGrand Theft Auto V is the second best-selling video game ever made. As pre-orders open for its successor, here is what the numbers say about how big GTA 6 could really be.

7 reasons why you must file ITR even if your income falls below exemption limitThink your income is too low to require an ITR? Not necessarily. Certain financial transactions can make return filing mandatory even if your income is below the exemption limit.

Explained: Why this investor calls India's stock market a poison marketAfter years of making money in the stock market, ace investor Shankar Sharma has delivered one of his strongest warnings yet. Here's why he believes Dalal Street is becoming a poison market.

entertainment.headline: No Bollywood comedy can top Welcome. Bolne de... bechare ko takleef hua hai As Welcome to the Jungle gears up for release, it's impossible not to look back at Welcome – Bollywood's happiest accident of chaos. Nearly 20 years later, its dialogues are still quoted, its scenes are still meme material, and Majnu Bhai is still very much an artist.

Is Rajinikanth's Dharman a remake of The Good Doctor? Director breaks silence Director Ashwath Marimuthu has addressed social media claims that Rajinikanth's Dharman is an adaptation of The Good Doctor. The clarification comes as the film's doctor-themed tagline and medical setting continue to fuel online speculation.

Raja Shivaji on OTT: When and where to watch Riteish Deshmukh's historical-drama Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji has started streaming on Netflix in Marathi and Hindi. The OTT release extends the reach of the record-breaking Marathi hit beyond its theatrical run.

Kangana Ranaut says families should not be judged in Pune fort murder case Actor Kangana Ranaut has voiced that families should not be blamed for the actions of their children in the Pune fort murder case, emphasising the influence of social media, AI, and peer pressure.

Woman tells SRK, 'I love you more than my husband'; his reply is pure king energy Shah Rukh Khan left fans in splits after jokingly responding to a woman who said she loved him more than her husband during an event in Mangaluru. The video has since gone viral on social media.

50 vanity vans, 900 people: Ahmed Khan breaks down the scale of Welcome 3 shoot Director Ahmed Khan has opened up about the scale of mounting Welcome To The Jungle with 34 actors and hundreds on set. The film hit theatres on June 26.

Heartin review: Sananth-Madonna Sebastian's love triangle never finds its sparkRelease Date:Fri Jun 26, 2026 Heartin movie review: Director Kishore Kumar's Heartin, starring Sananth, Madonna Sebastian and Emaya, is a romantic drama that explores modern relationships and second chances. The film took a formulaic approach and could have benefited from better performances from its women.

SS Rajamouli's Varanasi glimpse gets loud cheers at Annecy film fest. Viral video The first glimpse of director SS Rajamouli's Varanasi was unveiled at Annecy International Film Festival and drew loud cheers. The response has added to the buzz around the Mahesh Babu-led film ahead of its 2027 release.

Actor Sanchita Ugale's last rites take place 9 days after her suicide Sanchita Ugale's last rites were held in her ancestral village nine days after her death. Her family and colleagues have offered differing accounts as the police investigation continues.

Exclusive: Sohail Khan is 1st wildcard in Kunal Kemmu's reality show Alliance Sohail Khan is set to enter Prime Video's Alliance as one of its first wildcard contestants. His reported arrival could sharpen interest further, especially amid rumours that Seema Sajdeh is also on the show.

Mirzapur The Movie teaser X reviews: Fans say this film can challenge Dhurandhar The teaser of Mirzapur: The Movie has received a largely positive response on X, with fans praising Munna Tripathi's return and the nostalgic vibe. While many are excited for the theatrical adaptation, some questioned whether the film offers a fresh story.

Kajal Aggarwal, Shreyas Talpade's The India Story teaser tackles pesticide farming The teaser of The India Story: Slow Poison in Progress introduces Kajal Aggarwal and Shreyas Talpade as they confront pesticide farming. The film frames the issue as a public health concern and seeks to raise awareness about food safety.

Reese Witherspoon poses with Kerala Story 2 actor Aditi Bhatia. Pics Hollywood star Reese Witherspoon recently posed with The Kerala Story 2 actor Aditi Bhatia at a New York event for Elle, the Legally Blonde prequel. Their pink-themed appearance echoed the franchise's signature style ahead of the series launch.

Ann Blyth, Oscar-nominated Mildred Pierce actor, dies at 98 Ann Blyth, the Oscar-nominated actor who played Veda in Mildred Pierce, has died at 98. Her career spanned Broadway, films, musicals, television and operatic performance.

Rhea Chakraborty says can't get over trauma after Sushant Singh Rajput's death Actor Rhea Chakraborty said she still cannot get over the trauma following Sushant Singh Rajput's death, calling healing an ongoing process. The actor also spoke about living with PTSD alongside brother Shovik Chakraborty and rebuilding their lives after years of public scrutiny.

Shweta Tripathi on Mirzapur The Movie: Was confused about going back to being Golu Actor Shweta Tripathi said returning to season one Golu for Mirzapur: The Movie was initially confusing. The shift back to earlier character dynamics after years of revenge made the transition challenging.

Singer David Clayton-Thomas, voice of Blood, Sweat & Tears, dies at 84 David Clayton-Thomas, the iconic voice of Blood, Sweat & Tears, has died at 84. Here's what is known about his life, music career and legacy.

Supergirl review: DC's boldest new hero deserves a better filmRelease Date:Fri Jun 26, 2026 Actor Milly Alcock leads DC's bold new chapter with a fresh take on Kara Zor-El, but does the film live up to its promise? Read on to find out whether this emotional superhero adventure soars or falls short.

Welcome To The Jungle X review: Akshay Kumar saves this wild comedy Actor Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle opened in theatres to largely positive reactions on X. Viewers praised its family-entertainer appeal and cast chemistry, even as some flagged weak humour and an overcrowded screenplay.

general news.headline: Ketan wore hair patch, Siya knew of it: Father amid swirling murder theoriesKetan Agarwal's use of a wig has emerged as a fresh angle in the Pune murder case, though police say it alone cannot explain the motive behind Siya's decision to kill him.

India FTA terms changed covertly to block immigrants, says New Zealand ministerNew Zealand's ruling party has covertly changed the terms of the Free Trade Agreement (FTA) signed with India to prevent the immigration of Indian students and workers, alleged Winston Peters, the foreign minister and head of New Zealand First. Peters's party is part of the coalition headed by the National Party, which denied his claims.

Govt reveals names of 6 soldiers killed in action during Operation SindoorThe names of the six personnel have been added to the Roll of Honour on the National War Memorial website and inscribed on Wall 3D at the memorial in New Delhi, where they will stand alongside those of thousands of soldiers who made the supreme sacrifice in service of the nation.

Saayoni Ghosh is not a paltu, voters are OG paltuThe voters of Saayoni Ghosh's Jadavpur constituency flipped before she did. They are the original paltus. And, what does a true representative of the people do if not swing with the voters' mood?

Pak digs its Indic roots for Indus waters, gets some Aussie, British helpBritish and Australian envoys have recently highlighted Pakistan's heritage through funding and a Taxila visit. The timing is suspicious. Islamabad has been increasingly invoking the Indus Valley Civilisation during the Indus waters dispute with India.

Why 35°C in Britain feels unbearable than 45°C heatwave in IndiaBritain's heatwave has revived comparisons with India's far higher summer temperatures. Experts say humidity, heat-trapping homes and poor acclimatisation make lower temperatures feel harsher.

Ram Mandir trust chief Champat Rai quits day after arrests in donation theft caseThe resignations follow growing scrutiny after the SIT's preliminary report reportedly identified major lapses in managing and monitoring donations made by devotees at the Ram Temple.

Where is monsoon? Satellite captures rain clouds missing over IndiaSatellite imagery shows large parts of India without the deep rain clouds expected in late June despite the monsoon's advance. Forecasters say a developing system over the eastern Indian Ocean could revive rainfall, though its timing remains uncertain.

India loses key Bangladesh port project to China. Should Delhi be concerned?Bangladesh has signed a deal with China to build an economic zone near Mongla Port on land once allotted to India. The move deepens Dhaka's engagement with Beijing, while raising India's strategic concerns in the Bay of Bengal.

Preamble, secularism removed from NCERT's revised Class 9 Social Science textbookNCERT's revised Class 9 Social Science textbook under NEP 2020 adds a chapter on the 1975–77 Emergency while removing the Preamble and references to "secular" and "secularism" from the text. The revised book also changes how the Constitution and the Election Commission are explained.

Congress seeks probe into IPS officer Vishwas Nangare Patil's speech at RSS eventThe Maharashtra Congress has sought an inquiry into IPS officer Vishwas Nangare Patil's speech at a Hindu Sakal Samaj event. The party says his remarks on the RSS raise questions over political neutrality and service rules.

Mamata says no pardon for TMC defectors: Betraying party like abandoning motherMamata Banerjee accused defectors of abandoning workers to protect their own interests and urged party cadres to stay united amid political pressure. She also attacked the BJP-led Centre over alleged oppression and used the address to rally support ahead of the TMC's July 21 Martyrs' Day programme.

Hitler killed Jews; Jews used Hitler's weapons to defeat ArabsOne of history's greatest ironies unfolded in 1948. Weapons originally designed for Hitler's Nazi Germany, which killed nearly 6 million Jews, ended up helping defend the newly formed Jewish state of Israel. Jews sourced Nazi-era weapons and planes from Czechoslovakia, and beat back the armies of five Arab states.

Denmark plans azaan ban. It fears turning into a 'suburb of Islamabad'Denmark has revived plans for a nationwide ban on the Islamic call to prayer, coming months after the European country outlawed the Islamic full-face veil in public spaces. The proposal comes amid concerns over growing Islamisation in Denmark.

AI reality check hits Asia as Seoul's Kospi crashes 8%, Tokyo's Nikkei tumbles 5%South Korea's KOSPI fell 8.2%, prompting authorities to halt program trading for 20 minutes after steep losses rippled across the market. Japan's Nikkei 225 dropped 5%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 2.4%.

How a missing passport trapped a man inside an airport for 18 yearsFor 18 years, Iran-born Mehran Karimi Nasseri lived inside Paris's Charles de Gaulle Airport after becoming trapped in a bureaucratic nightmare. His extraordinary story inspired Steven Spielberg's film The Terminal and turned an ordinary transit lounge into one of the strangest addresses in modern history.

Shoppers rush for AC coolers in France as soon as store opens amid brutal heatwaveA video from a store in Chambery showed shoppers scrambling through aisles and crowding around cooling appliances in a frantic attempt to secure relief from the soaring temperatures. Amid visible tension, the scene spiralled into chaos as stunned onlookers watched.

Pune fort murder: Boyfriend Chetan persuaded Siya to kill fiance Ketan, police sayPolice said questioning revealed that Chetan Chaudhary urged Siya Goyal to eliminate Ketan Agarwal at Lohagad Fort. The claim shifts focus to the alleged conspiracy as investigators examine family statements and the events surrounding the June 18 trip.

Ram Mandir donation row: SIT rules out silver theft amid Sanjay Raut's allegationSanjay Raut questioned the alleged disappearance of a 4-kg silver brick donated for Ram Mandir in Ayodhya and demanded an explanation from the temple trust, alleging no receipt or update was given post-donation.

The Coaching Apocalypse: Do we really need them?Kota's suicides and repeated exam paper leaks raise a difficult question: is India's coaching culture solving a problem or creating one? As pressure mounts for education reform, many argue the real issue isn't coaching: it's the exam system itself.

Rajasthan woman cop puts feet on desk as elderly complainant waits, suspendedA Rajasthan woman constable was suspended after a viral photograph showed her with both feet on a desk while an elderly man waited to lodge a complaint.

ED seizes Rs 3.66 crore life insurance policies in Rs 1,200-crore SBI fraud caseThe ED has attached two Zurich International Life insurance policies linked to Shrikant Bhasi in the AOPL case. The move follows findings that the policies were funded through connected overseas entities and were being readied for surrender.

Prove it's a mask: Canada-based NRI challenges Bhagwant Mann as video row escalatesCanada-based NRI Jagman Samra has strongly denied Bhagwant Mann's allegation that he used a hyper-realistic mask to stage the controversial video and has challenged him to produce concrete evidence to back his claims.

There are many ways to feel like an outsider. Mine began with passport verificationAfter the MEA said passports are only travel documents, I was reminded of my police verification that turned into an interrogation over my nationality. If a passport is just a travel document, how do you justify the cumbersome process to get one?

sports.headline: Who will Serena Williams face on her Slam comeback? Wimbledon draw explained Wimbledon 2026: Serena Williams will begin her campaign against Australia's rising star Maya Joint after receiving a wildcard for her first Grand Slam singles appearance since the 2022 US Open.

Wimbledon draw: Novak Djokovic in Sinner's half, faces tough road to quarters Wimbledon 2026: Novak Djokovic has landed in Jannik Sinner's half of the draw. Carlos Alcaraz's withdrawal opens the field, but a demanding route still shapes Djokovic's bid for a 25th Grand Slam title.

1st T20I Live: India win toss and opt to bowl first as Sooryavanshi misses out Ireland (IRE) vs India (IND) Live Score, 1st T20I: India's new captain has won the toss in Belfast and opted to bowl first, however, no Sooryavanshi this time around.

Ecuador coach's euphoric celebration after mega-upset vs Germany goes viral Ecuador coach Sebastian Beccacece's emotional celebration after his side's dramatic 2-1 win over Germany went viral as the South American nation reached the FIFA World Cup knockout stage for the first time since 2006. Beccacece sprinted into the stands to embrace his wife and supporters after Ecuador completed a remarkable turnaround.

Germany camp divided? Undav, Kimmich contradict Nagelsmann after Ecuador defeat Germany manager Julian Nagelsmann dismissed suggestions of a lack of desire as "nonsense", but captain Joshua Kimmich and striker Deniz Undav painted a very different picture after their side's 2-1 defeat to Ecuador in their final Group E match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

Let's play like Lions: Shreyas Iyer delivers first huddle talk as T20I captain Shreyas Iyer addressed India's huddle in Belfast before his first T20I as captain. His message centred on culture, support and creating a fun team environment as a new leadership phase begins.

Bengal T20 League is creating the next IPL stars: Shahbaz Ahmed The Bengal T20 League has emerged as a key platform for young cricketers looking to break into the IPL and Bengal's senior teams. Speaking to India Today, Rarh Tigers captain Shahbaz Ahmed urged youngsters to focus on performances rather than IPL contracts, saying selectors and scouts are already watching the tournament closely.

Was 16-year-old Sachin as viral as Sooryavanshi before his India debut? Long before algorithms and reels, a 16-year-old Sachin Tendulkar captivated India without a single smartphone in sight. As Vaibhav Sooryavanshi debuts, we revisit an analogue era when a nation fell for a myth built on whispers.

Barely past the group stage, FIFA World Cup 2026 rewrites goalscoring records The FIFA World Cup 2026 has already entered the record books. Barely past the group stage, the expanded tournament became the highest-scoring edition in history. Lionel Messi leads the goalscoring charts at the moment with 5 strikes to his name.

Australia advance after Paraguay draw as USA top Group D despite Turkey defeat FIFA World Cup: Australia booked their place in the Round of 32 after holding Paraguay to a hard-fought 0-0 draw in their final Group D match on Thursday, while the United States finished top of the group despite suffering a dramatic 3-2 defeat to Turkey in Los Angeles.

Anushka Yadav's heavy metal miracle at The KalingaAt the Kalinga Stadium, 18-year-old Anushka Yadav broke the women's hammer throw national record twice in one night, throwing 67.02m to qualify for the 2026 Asian Games. She announced herself as one of India's brightest field-event prospects.

Man City agree club-record deal with Forest to sign World Cup star Elliot AndersonManchester City have agreed a club-record deal worth £116 million with Nottingham Forest to sign England midfielder Elliot Anderson, with the 23-year-old set to undergo a medical before completing his move to the Etihad Stadium.

National holiday declared in Ecuador after country qualifies for WC knockoutsEcuador declared a national holiday after their dramatic 2-1 comeback win over Germany which sealed them a place in the FIFA World Cup knockout rounds for the first time in 20 years. President Daniel Noboa hailed the team's resilience in a difficult campaign.

Netherlands top Group F as Japan and Sweden reach knockoutsThe Netherlands sealed top spot in Group F with a 3-1 win over Tunisia, while Japan booked their place in the last 32 after a 1-1 draw with Sweden. Sweden also progressed as one of the best third-placed teams, setting up knockout ties for both the Dutch and Japan.

Sooryavanshi hype overshadows Shreyas Iyer's debut as India captain in BelfastIndia begin a new T20I era under captain Shreyas Iyer against Ireland in Belfast, with attention split between leadership transition and 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's potential debut. Ireland, led by Lorcan Tucker, also eye opportunities for young players as both teams deal with transitions and challenging playing conditions.

Australia reach knockouts, USA top Group D despite loss to TurkeyAustralia (AUS) vs Paraguay (PAR) highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: USA and Australia qualify for the Round of 32 of the FIFA World Cup. Australia held Paraguay to a 0-0 draw, while the USA lost to Turkey 2-3 in a last-gasp goal by Kaan Ayhan. Despite the loss, USA top Group D.

Iran seek knockout berth as coach Amir Ghalenoei avoids Pride celebration debateIran aim to make their nation proud against Egypt with a place in the World Cup knockout stages on the line, coach Amir Ghalenoei said on Thursday, while refusing to be drawn into the debate over the Pride celebrations surrounding the match.

FIFA World Cup 2026 shatters attendance record despite US travel messThe 2026 FIFA World Cup has become the most-attended edition in history, crossing 3.6 million spectators with nearly half the tournament still to play. The milestone arrives despite criticism over expensive tickets, visa hurdles and logistical concerns across the United States.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut faces rain threat in Belfast? Here's the weather forecastIND vs IRE, 1st T20I: Rain could threaten India's first T20I against Ireland in Belfast, with weather forecasts predicting intermittent showers. The weather may also delay Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's much-anticipated India debut.

TUN 1-3 NED Highlights: Dutch finish top of Group F, Japan and Sweden go throughNetherlands (NED) vs Tunisia (TUN), FIFA World Cup Highlights: (TUN 1-3 NED) The Netherlands topped Group F with a 3-1 win over Tunisia, while Japan secured second place after a 1-1 draw with Sweden. Sweden also progressed as one of the tournament's best third-placed teams.

Ecuador script stunning Germany upset to keep World Cup dream aliveFIFA World Cup 2026: Ecuador came from behind to produce one of the biggest shocks of the 2026 World Cup, stunning Germany 2-1 in New Jersey. The comeback secured Sebastián Beccacece's side a place in the knockout stages as one of the tournament's best third-placed teams.

Nicolas Pepe's brace closes curtain on Curacao, creates history for Ivory CoastNicolas Pepe struck twice as Ivory Coast beat Curacao 2-0 to reach the FIFA World Cup knockout stage for the first time. Curacao bowed out after an inspiring debut campaign despite a spirited display.

FIFA World Cup 2026: Seattle's Pride Match puts Egypt vs Iran clash in spotlightFIFA said fans can carry rainbow flags into the Egypt-Iran World Cup match in Seattle. The decision has sharpened attention on Pride celebrations opposed by both teams' federations.

Animesh Kujur leads India's Asiad qualification charge on Day 2Animesh Kujur and several others met the AFI's Asian Games standards on Day 2 of the Inter-State Championships. Sindhushree G's national record in the pole vault underlined the growing competition for India's Aichi-Nagoya squad.

Mohammad Amir starts afresh as British citizen, joins T20 Blast as domestic playerMohammad Amir has begun a new phase of his career after joining Notts Outlaws in the T20 Blast as a domestic player following the acquisition of British citizenship, allowing him to play county cricket without occupying an overseas slot.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to get India debut in Belfast T20I? Batting coach breaks silenceIndia batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will get his chance when the time is right, insisting the team will not drop an in-form player just to hand the 15-year-old his debut.

ECU 2-1 GER Highlights: Ecuador upset Germany, Ivory Coast advanceEcuador (ECU) vs Germany (GER) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (ECU 2-1 GER) Ecuador refused to let their World Cup dream die, overturning an early deficit to stun Germany and sneak into the knockout stages as one of the best third-placed teams. In the other Group E clash, Ivory Coast also made history by reaching the last 32 for the first time.

South Africa vs Netherlands: Live Score and Updates from BristolSA-W vs NED-W, Women's T20 World Cup: Live Score and Updates: Netherlands have won the toss and opted to bowl first at County Ground in Bristol. South Africa will be looking to put up a strong show against the Dutch and strengthen their chances of qualifying for the semis.

Untidy India scrape past Bangladesh to keep semifinal hopes aliveWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs BAN: India beat Bangladesh by five wickets in Manchester on Thursday, but will need to improve significantly in several areas of their game before facing Australia in a crucial Group A clash at Lord's on Sunday.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
